title: Critical Bugfix Deploy
summary: Full audit found 12 bugs (4 critical). Order duplication: match_results never marked processed, same signal traded 4x. Bracket cashout: take-profit/stop-loss only updated DB, never sold on CLOB — positions up 228% sat unsold. CLOB minimum: orders below 5 shares rejected but retried every 5 min. FK enforcement missing. All fixed, 25 tests passing, mandatory audit checklist added.

title: Day 3: Overnight Spending Spree
summary: Agent deployed 7 trades at midnight totaling ~$123, draining wallet from $134 to $11. Positions: Somaliland Abraham Accords (NO at 62%), Hormuz ship transit (YES at 28%), Trump-Putin meeting (YES at 86%), Silver commodity (NO at 48%), Opensea FDV (NO at 35%), Trump AG picks (YES at 20-26%). More diverse than before — politics, commodities, crypto regulation, geopolitics — but bet sizing too aggressive at 10% per trade. Seven trades × $18 = wallet emptied in one cycle. Learning loop shows 32W/82L overall (28% win rate). The system is finding diverse opportunities but needs smaller position sizes to survive losing streaks. Key lesson: diversification means nothing if you size each bet at 10% and place 7 at once.

title: Day 2: New Rules, Hard Lessons
body:
Two days in and the agent has been through a lot. Started with $245.80, currently sitting around $400+ thanks almost entirely to a lucky Musk tweet bracket play.

What happened: The agent's arbitrage scanner found Elon Musk tweet-count brackets trading at 1-2 cents each and bought several. When Musk's actual pace landed near the 420-439 and 460-479 ranges, those brackets went from pennies to 8-10 cents — a 5-8x return. That single accidental play accounts for nearly all the gains.

What went wrong: Everything else. 114 trades with an 8% win rate. Crypto coin flips (58 trades, pure gambling), hallucinated sports edges ("Hungary election impacts FK Rostov"), ETH price brackets that imploded, and a weather bet on Seoul temperature. The data was clear: the agent was losing money on everything except the lucky bracket hit.

What changed today: Deployed data-driven rules based on analyzing all 114 historical trades. Key findings — trades under 30% entry price had 0% win rate, small bets outperformed large ones by 2.5x. Removed the category whitelist that was limiting us to only Iran/Trump markets. The agent can now trade across all Polymarket categories — politics, sports, entertainment, tech — wherever news creates genuine edge.

Strategy going forward: All limits are now percentage-based (scales with capital), no artificial caps on trade count or positions. The blacklist blocks known garbage (crypto coinflips, weather, tweet counts) while the strict LLM prompts and Claude review catch hallucinated reasoning. Focus is diversification across many small, well-reasoned bets rather than concentration on one thesis.

The Musk brackets resolve April 17. If the 420-439 range hits, that's an $800+ payday on a $13 cost basis. We'll see.
summary: Deployed data-driven rules after analyzing 114 trades. Removed category whitelist, added learning loop. Agent now trades across all Polymarket categories.
